chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 19: Advanced Patterns and Techniques
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the nineteenth chapter of the IVD book. Covers
# advanced IVD patterns: versioning, microservices, MCP integration, and
# provides the deep-dive treatment for the three experimental sections
# (roles, authorship, evaluation) introduced in earlier chapters.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_4_adoption.chapters[2]
# Source:    ivd_system_intent.yaml (experimental sections), framework.md, recipes/
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Equip the reader with advanced IVD patterns — versioning, microservices coordination, MCP integration, AI-editable configuration — and deliver the comprehensive deep-dive on the three experimental sections: dynamic roles, intent authorship, and continuous evaluation."
  
  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Can version intent artifacts using semantic versioning adapted for understanding
    2. Can coordinate shared intents across microservices boundaries
    3. Understands intent-driven API contracts and advanced verification (schema validation, linting)
    4. Knows how to integrate IVD with MCP (Model Context Protocol) for universal AI access
    5. Understands AI-editable configuration patterns (Cline-style)
    6. Has a comprehensive understanding of dynamic roles: how a single agent switches behavioral modes based on context, with constraints, tool subsets, and verification per role (the full treatment of the experimental roles section)
    7. Has a comprehensive understanding of intent authorship: the trust boundary spectrum from human_directed → ai_proposed → ai_autonomous, with oversight models and escalation (the full treatment of the experimental authorship section)
    8. Has a comprehensive understanding of continuous evaluation: the improvement loop beyond pass/fail verification — evaluate → adjust → re-verify, bounded iteration, protected fields, and escalation (the full treatment of the experimental evaluation section)
    
    This is the advanced chapter. The reader has built projects (Part II), seen evidence (Part III), and started adopting (Ch17–18). Now they learn the patterns that unlock IVD's full power. Sections 19.7–19.9 are where the experimental sections — first demonstrated in Ch5, Ch6, and Ch9 — get their comprehensive explanation, rationale, and guidance.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can apply semantic versioning to intent artifacts and knows when to bump major/minor/patch
    - Reader can design shared intents for microservices coordi

protected fields, and escalation (the full treatment of the experimental evaluation section)
    
    This is the advanced chapter. The reader has built projects (Part II), seen evidence (Part III), and started adopting (Ch17–18). Now they learn the patterns that unlock IVD's full power. Sections 19.7–19.9 are where the experimental sections — first demonstrated in Ch5, Ch6, and Ch9 — get their comprehensive explanation, rationale, and guidance.
  
  success_metric: |
    - Reader can apply semantic versioning to intent artifacts and knows when to bump major/minor/patch
    - Reader can design shared intents for microservices coordination (parent intents, contract intents)
    - Reader understands MCP integration with IVD (tools exposed via interface section, intent-driven tool surface)
    - Reader can implement dynamic roles for a single-agent system with per-role constraints and verification
    - Reader understands the authorship spectrum and can declare appropriate trust boundaries for AI-originated intent
    - Reader can design an evaluation loop with criteria, bounded iterations, protected fields, and escalation
    - Sections 19.7–19.9 match ivd_system_intent.yaml experimental section definitions exactly
    - Chapter ends with pattern recognition bridge to Chapter 20 (IVD Ecosystem)
  
  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4 purpose: adopt and scale IVD)"
    - "ivd_system_intent.yaml (experimental sections: roles, authorship, evaluation)"
    - "framework.md (canonical IVD patterns)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch19)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "19.1"
      title: "Versioning Intent Artifacts (Semantic Versioning for Understanding)"
      required_content:
        - "Why intent artifacts need versioning: as systems evolve, intent evolves — changelog tracks history but version numbers communicate the nature of change"
        - "Semantic versioning adapted for intent: MAJOR (goal or constraints change — breaking), MINOR (new sections, new constraints — additive), PATCH (clarifications, typos, rationale updates — non-functional)"
        - "Version field placement and conventions: version in implementation section, referenced in changelog"
        - "When to bump: clear decision criteria with examples (constraint threshold change = MAJOR; new risk added = MINOR; rationale clarified = PATCH)"
        - "Version references between artifacts: recipe_version in intent artifacts, parent intent version pinning for stability"
        - "Version history as institutional memory: how changelog + version numbers tell the story of a system's evolution"
    
    - id: "19.2"
      title: "Shared Intents Across Services (Microservices Coordination)"
      required_content:
        - "The microservices intent challenge: each service has its own intent, but services depend on each other — how to coordinate without coupling"
        - "Shared parent intents: system-level intent that multiple ser

ed = MINOR; rationale clarified = PATCH)"
        - "Version references between artifacts: recipe_version in intent artifacts, parent intent version pinning for stability"
        - "Version history as institutional memory: how changelog + version numbers tell the story of a system's evolution"
    
    - id: "19.2"
      title: "Shared Intents Across Services (Microservices Coordination)"
      required_content:
        - "The microservices intent challenge: each service has its own intent, but services depend on each other — how to coordinate without coupling"
        - "Shared parent intents: system-level intent that multiple services reference via parent_intent, inheriting architecture principles and conventions"
        - "Contract intents at service boundaries: intent artifacts that define the agreement between two services (API contract, event schema, shared constraints)"
        - "Multi-team coordination: how teams share intent without merge conflicts — conventions for shared vs local constraints"
        - "Cross-service verification: how to verify that changes in Service A don't violate intents in Service B"
        - "Real-world example: two microservices sharing a lead qualification contract intent"
    
    - id: "19.3"
      title: "Intent-Driven API Contracts"
      required_content:
        - "From OpenAPI to intent-driven contracts: API specs define shape, intent defines meaning and constraints"
        - "The interface section as API contract: how the interface optional section (type: api) documents the callable surface with verifiable constraints"
        - "Consumer-driven contracts with IVD: consumers declare what they need from the API in their own intents; provider intent satisfies consumer constraints"
        - "Breaking change detection: version bumps + constraint tests catch API changes that break consumers"
        - "Example: REST API with intent-driven contract showing constraints, versioning, and consumer verification"
    
    - id: "19.4"
      title: "Advanced Verification: Schema Validation and Linting"
      required_content:
        - "Beyond basic constraint tests: schema validation for intent artifacts themselves (does this YAML match the IVD schema?)"
        - "Intent linting: automated checks for common mistakes (missing test paths, empty constraints, orphaned evidence links)"
        - "Custom verification rules: teams defining project-specific verification beyond the IVD defaults"
        - "Integration with CI/CD: schema validation and linting as pre-commit hooks and PR checks"
        - "The ivd_validate tool: how it works, what it checks, how to extend it"
        - "Progressive verification: start with basic checks, add schema validation, then add custom rules as team matures"
    
    - id: "19.5"
      title: "MCP Integration: Universal Protocol for AI Access"
      required_content:
        - "What MCP (Model Context Protocol) is and why it matters for IVD: a universal protocol for AI agents to access tools and resources"
        - "IVD + MCP: how intent artifacts document MCP tool surfaces (interface section with type: mcp)"
        - "MCP server intent artifacts: how to create intent for a

checks"
        - "The ivd_validate tool: how it works, what it checks, how to extend it"
        - "Progressive verification: start with basic checks, add schema validation, then add custom rules as team matures"
    
    - id: "19.5"
      title: "MCP Integration: Universal Protocol for AI Access"
      required_content:
        - "What MCP (Model Context Protocol) is and why it matters for IVD: a universal protocol for AI agents to access tools and resources"
        - "IVD + MCP: how intent artifacts document MCP tool surfaces (interface section with type: mcp)"
        - "MCP server intent artifacts: how to create intent for an MCP server, documenting each tool with parameters, returns, and tests"
        - "The IVD MCP server itself: how IVD tools (validate, scaffold, search, assess_coverage) are exposed via MCP"
        - "Pattern: any module that exposes tools to AI agents should have an interface section — MCP makes this universal"
        - "Example: MCP server with intent artifact showing tool surface documentation"
    
    - id: "19.6"
      title: "AI Agents That Improve Their Own Intent"
      required_content:
        - "AI-editable configuration (Cline-style): agents that can modify their own intent artifacts within declared boundaries"
        - "The self-improvement pattern: agent runs → evaluates output → proposes intent modification → verification confirms no constraint violations → modification applied"
        - "Guardrails for self-modification: which sections the AI can modify (implementation, verification) vs which require human approval (goal, constraints)"
        - "Connection to authorship (19.8) and evaluation (19.9): self-improvement requires both authority declaration and bounded improvement loops"
        - "When self-modification is appropriate: autonomous workflows, production agents with feedback loops"
        - "When self-modification is dangerous: safety-critical systems, regulatory contexts, early-stage agents without sufficient guardrails"
    
    - id: "19.7"
      title: "Dynamic Roles: Context-Dependent Agent Behavior"
      required_content:
        - "THE DEEP DIVE on the roles experimental section (first demonstrated in Ch5, referenced in subsequent chapters)"
        - "What dynamic roles solve: a single agent that behaves differently depending on context (implementer, reviewer, architect, teacher, debugger) — each mode with distinct constraints, tool subsets, and verification"
        - "The roles section structure: default role, switching mechanism (user_directed | context_inferred | explicit_command), role definitions with name, description, when, constraints, tools, verification"
        - "Relationship to interface: interface = WHAT the agent can do (tools); roles = HOW it behaves in different contexts. A role may restrict which interface tools are available."
        - "Relationship to coordinator pattern: roles is for ONE agent with MULTIPLE modes; coordinator recipe is for MULTIPLE agents with their own intents. They are compatible: coordinator routes to agents that each have roles."
        - "When to use roles vs separate agents: use roles when modes share most capabilities and diff

| context_inferred | explicit_command), role definitions with name, description, when, constraints, tools, verification"
        - "Relationship to interface: interface = WHAT the agent can do (tools); roles = HOW it behaves in different contexts. A role may restrict which interface tools are available."
        - "Relationship to coordinator pattern: roles is for ONE agent with MULTIPLE modes; coordinator recipe is for MULTIPLE agents with their own intents. They are compatible: coordinator routes to agents that each have roles."
        - "When to use roles vs separate agents: use roles when modes share most capabilities and differ in constraints/access; use separate agents when modes have fundamentally different tool sets or knowledge"
        - "Verification per role: each role links to a test that verifies behavioral constraints are respected (e.g., reviewer is read-only; implementer writes intent before code)"
        - "Real-world example: Cursor IDE modes (agent, plan, debug, ask) as a roles-based design — each mode has different constraints and available tools"
    
    - id: "19.8"
      title: "Intent Authorship: Trust Boundaries for AI-Originated Intent"
      required_content:
        - "THE DEEP DIVE on the authorship experimental section (first demonstrated in Ch6/Ch9, referenced in subsequent chapters)"
        - "The fundamental question: as AI agents become more autonomous — designing workflows, proposing structures, creating sub-intents — who originated this intent, and what can the AI change without asking?"
        - "The authorship section structure: origin (human_directed | ai_proposed | ai_autonomous), human_oversight (review_required | audit_trail | escalation_only), ai_authority (can_create, can_modify, requires_approval), escalation rules"
        - "The three authorship levels explained in depth: human_directed (standard IVD, no authorship needed), ai_proposed (AI proposes, human reviews before activation), ai_autonomous (AI operates within declared boundaries, human audits after)"
        - "Relationship to roles: roles define HOW an agent behaves; authorship defines WHAT authority that agent has over intent itself. An agent in 'architect' role may have authority to create intents; in 'implementer' role it may only modify implementation sections."
        - "Relationship to evaluation: authorship is the governance layer for the evaluation loop — adjustment.authority must respect ai_authority boundaries"
        - "Designing trust boundaries: how to decide which fields are AI-modifiable vs human-approved (risk-based: higher-risk fields require more oversight)"
        - "Real-world example: autonomous workflow agent with ai_proposed authorship — creates sub-intents but requires human review before activation"
    
    - id: "19.9"
      title: "Continuous Evaluation: The Improvement Loop"
      required_content:
        - "THE DEEP DIVE on the evaluation experimental section (first demonstrated in Ch9, referenced in subsequent chapters)"
        - "The key distinction: verification is binary (pass/fail), evaluation is continuous (how good is it, and how can it improve?). Verification is a gate; evaluation is a loo

vs human-approved (risk-based: higher-risk fields require more oversight)"
        - "Real-world example: autonomous workflow agent with ai_proposed authorship — creates sub-intents but requires human review before activation"
    
    - id: "19.9"
      title: "Continuous Evaluation: The Improvement Loop"
      required_content:
        - "THE DEEP DIVE on the evaluation experimental section (first demonstrated in Ch9, referenced in subsequent chapters)"
        - "The key distinction: verification is binary (pass/fail), evaluation is continuous (how good is it, and how can it improve?). Verification is a gate; evaluation is a loop."
        - "The evaluation section structure: criteria (metric, target, source), adjustment (authority, scope, protected), cycle (trigger, max_iterations, stop_when, escalate_when)"
        - "Evaluation criteria design: measurable quality metrics (not aspirational) — output_quality_score, constraint_pass_rate, latency, user_satisfaction"
        - "Adjustment authority and protected fields: what CAN be adjusted (implementation, workflow_steps) vs what CANNOT (intent.goal, constraints). Evaluation NEVER relaxes constraints — constraints are the floor."
        - "Bounded iteration: max_iterations prevents infinite loops; stop_when defines success; escalate_when triggers human intervention"
        - "Relationship to authorship: the evaluation loop's adjustment.authority must respect the authorship section's ai_authority. If authorship says AI cannot modify constraints, evaluation cannot relax them."
        - "Relationship to constraints: constraints are binary guardrails; evaluation criteria are continuous quality targets. Evaluation improves quality above the constraint floor."
    
  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch19 key_points: versioning, multi-team coordination, microservices, AI-editable config, dynamic roles, intent authorship, continuous evaluation"
      - "At least one complete YAML example for each experimental section (roles, authorship, evaluation) inline in sections 19.7–19.9"
      - "Prose-led writing style (Part IV): prose explains and generalizes; examples illustrate"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Checklist in final section (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Clear bridge to Chapter 20 (IVD Ecosystem)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch19; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Intent artifact versioning and evolution — semantic versioning adapted for understanding: MAJOR (goals/constraints change), MINOR (additive), PATCH (clarification)"
  - "Multi-team coordination with shared intents — parent intents and contract intents enable microservices coordination without coupling"
  - "IVD for microservices architectures — shared parent intents, contract intents at service boundaries, cross-service verification"

============================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch19; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "Intent artifact versioning and evolution — semantic versioning adapted for understanding: MAJOR (goals/constraints change), MINOR (additive), PATCH (clarification)"
  - "Multi-team coordination with shared intents — parent intents and contract intents enable microservices coordination without coupling"
  - "IVD for microservices architectures — shared parent intents, contract intents at service boundaries, cross-service verification"
  - "AI-editable configuration (Cline-style) — agents that modify their own intent within declared boundaries, with guardrails for self-modification"
  - "Dynamic roles: single agents with context-dependent behavior (roles section, agent-role-based recipe) — the comprehensive treatment of when one agent needs multiple behavioral modes with distinct constraints and verification"
  - "Intent authorship: formalizing trust boundaries for AI-originated intent (authorship section) — the spectrum from human_directed to ai_autonomous, with oversight models and escalation"
  - "Continuous evaluation: the improvement loop beyond pass/fail verification (evaluation section) — evaluate → adjust → re-verify, bounded iteration, protected fields, and escalation"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and a pre-move-on checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep 'checklist\\|Before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"
  
  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"
  
  - name: "writing_style_part_4"
    requirement: "Prose leads; code and intent-artifact examples illustrate. Prose explains and generalizes."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose drives the narrative; YAML/code examples support and illustrate points, not lead them"
    test: "Each section begins with prose explanation before any code/YAML blocks; code blocks appear after prose establishes context"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"

tem_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose drives the narrative; YAML/code examples support and illustrate points, not lead them"
    test: "Each section begins with prose explanation before any code/YAML blocks; code blocks appear after prose establishes context"
  
  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"
  
  - name: "experimental_sections_accuracy"
    requirement: "Sections 19.7, 19.8, and 19.9 must match ivd_system_intent.yaml experimental section definitions exactly — structure, field names, relationships, promotion criteria, when_to_use/when_not_to_use"
    source: "ivd_system_intent.yaml (approved_optional_sections: roles, authorship, evaluation)"
    validation: "Diff chapter YAML examples and prose against ivd_system_intent.yaml section definitions; zero contradictions"
    test: "Every field name, relationship description, and structural element in 19.7–19.9 matches the canonical definition in ivd_system_intent.yaml; no invented fields or altered semantics"
  
  - name: "framework_alignment"
    requirement: "All patterns described in this chapter must align with framework.md — no contradictions with principles, optional sections, recipes, or level definitions"
    source: "framework.md (canonical IVD documentation)"
    validation: "Manual review: every pattern in Ch19 is consistent with framework.md"
    test: "No principle, pattern, or section description in chapter.md contradicts framework.md; cross-reference check produces zero conflicts"
  
  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch19 aligns with framework principles, system intent, and book intent; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, optional section, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
  
  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"
  
  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Any performance claims or comparative metrics must be marked as illustrative (author experience) unless backed by controlled study"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Metric/performance sections include caveat about illustrative data

es/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"
  
  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "Any performance claims or comparative metrics must be marked as illustrative (author experience) unless backed by controlled study"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Metric/performance sections include caveat about illustrative data"
    test: "Any section with numerical claims contains explicit caveat text (e.g., 'illustrative', 'author experience', 'not a controlled study')"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 19 is the advanced patterns chapter — combining infrastructure-level IVD patterns (versioning, microservices, MCP) with the comprehensive deep-dive on the three experimental sections (roles, authorship, evaluation). Part IV sequence: Ch17 getting started, Ch18 scaling, Ch19 advanced patterns, Ch20 ecosystem, Ch21 future."
  
  reasoning: |
    - Part IV purpose: adopt and scale IVD in teams and organizations. Ch19 is for teams that have adopted IVD and want to go deeper.
    - Versioning, microservices, and MCP integration are natural advanced topics: teams that have adopted IVD at single-project scale need patterns for multi-project, multi-team, and multi-service coordination.
    - Sections 19.7–19.9 are the deep-dive for the three experimental sections (roles, authorship, evaluation). These were introduced as demonstrations in Part II (Ch5 roles, Ch6/Ch9 authorship, Ch9 evaluation) but the full explanation — structure, relationships, when-to-use, design guidance — belongs here in the advanced chapter.
    - Placing the deep-dive in Part IV (not Part II) follows the book's pedagogy: Part II is "build this" with brief introductions; Part IV is "understand deeply and adopt" with comprehensive treatment.
    - AI-editable configuration (19.6) bridges to the experimental sections: self-improving agents require authorship boundaries (19.8) and evaluation loops (19.9).
  
  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_4, Ch19 key_points, placeholder); ivd_system_intent.yaml (experimental sections); framework.md (optional sections documentation)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Deep-dive experimental sections in Part II alongside first demonstrations"
    rejected_because: "Part II is 'build this' — learning by doing. Full theoretical treatment of experimental sections would break the flow. Readers need to see the sections in action first (Ch5/6/9), then understand them comprehensively later (Ch19)."
    when_it_works: "If readers skip Part IV and

on)"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Deep-dive experimental sections in Part II alongside first demonstrations"
    rejected_because: "Part II is 'build this' — learning by doing. Full theoretical treatment of experimental sections would break the flow. Readers need to see the sections in action first (Ch5/6/9), then understand them comprehensively later (Ch19)."
    when_it_works: "If readers skip Part IV and only read Part II. Mitigated by ensuring Ch5/6/9 demonstrations are self-sufficient for basic usage."
  
  - name: "Separate chapters for versioning/microservices and experimental deep-dives"
    rejected_because: "Would create two thin chapters instead of one substantial chapter. The advanced patterns and experimental deep-dives are thematically related (both are 'IVD at scale and depth') and benefit from being in one reference chapter."
    when_it_works: "If the chapter exceeds the target page count significantly; could be split if needed during editing."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Experimental sections drift from ivd_system_intent.yaml definitions during writing"
    action: "Cross-reference every field, relationship, and example in 19.7–19.9 against ivd_system_intent.yaml before finalizing; constraint experimental_sections_accuracy enforces this"
    monitor: "Verification cross-check against ivd_system_intent.yaml"
    severity: "high"
  
  - condition: "Chapter is too long — covers too many topics (versioning + microservices + MCP + 3 experimental deep-dives)"
    action: "Target 30–40 pages; keep versioning/microservices/MCP concise (patterns + examples); allocate proportionally more space to 19.7–19.9 deep-dives"
    monitor: "Page count; section length distribution"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Experimental sections may be promoted to canonical or changed before book publication"
    action: "Note experimental status explicitly; structure content so it works whether sections are still experimental or promoted by publication time"
    monitor: "ivd_system_intent.yaml changelog for promotion decisions"
    severity: "medium"
  
  - condition: "Readers who skipped Part II demonstrations of roles/authorship/evaluation arrive at 19.7–19.9 without context"
    action: "Each deep-dive section (19.7–19.9) should begin with a brief 'what this section adds' paragraph referencing the earlier demonstrations, making it accessible to readers coming from the Leader Path (Part I → Part III → Part IV)"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback from Leader Path readers"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-4-adoption/chapter-19-advanced-pat

hip/evaluation arrive at 19.7–19.9 without context"
    action: "Each deep-dive section (19.7–19.9) should begin with a brief 'what this section adds' paragraph referencing the earlier demonstrations, making it accessible to readers coming from the Leader Path (Part I → Part III → Part IV)"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback from Leader Path readers"
    severity: "low"

# ==============================================================================
# IMPLEMENTATION
# ==============================================================================

implementation:
  intent_artifact: "manuscript/part-4-adoption/chapter-19-advanced-patterns/chapter_19_advanced_patterns_intent.yaml"
  output: "manuscript/part-4-adoption/chapter-19-advanced-patterns/chapter.md"
  status: "pending"
  version: 1
  
  process:
    reference: "book/chapter_writing_process_intent.yaml"
    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapter_folder"
      - "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    pending_steps:
      - "step_4_write_manuscript"
      - "step_5_cross_check"
      - "step_6_update_chapter_status"
      - "step_7_update_book_intent"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, system intent, and framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch19 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (19.1–19.9) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and pre-move-on checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways"

      19.3 API contracts, 19.4 advanced verification, 19.5 MCP integration, 19.6 AI self-improvement,

      no_unsourced_statistics — all with test fields.
